channel.title: The Arunachal Times
channel.description: - Latest Arunachal News, North East News, National News, World News

item.title: Search intensifies for four people missing in flash flood
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: Search operations have been intensified for four people who remain missing following a flash flood in Keyi Panyor district, officials said on Friday. Rescue teams launched raft operations from the dam side of the Panyor Lower Hydroelectric Project on Friday morning to trace those who went missing from NEEPCO Colony during the […]

item.title: DoT enables intra-circle roaming facility in four dists
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) has activated intra-circle roaming (ICR) in four districts of Arunachal Pradesh for three days till 28 June to ensure uninterrupted mobile connectivity amid widespread disruption caused by heavy rainfall, cloudbursts and landslides, an official statement said on Friday. The facility has been enabled in Keyi Panyor, Lower […]

item.title: F&CS dept prioritises improved public service delivery
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: Strengthening the public distribution system (PDS), addressing manpower shortage, and improving public service delivery were among the key focus areas during the annual review meeting of the Food & Civil Supplies (F&CS) Department, held at DK convention hall here on Friday. Chairing the meeting, Food & Civil Supplies Minister Gabriel D Wangsu […]

item.title: Defunct school to reopen soon
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: [ Karda Natam ] DAPORIJO, 26 Jun: In a remarkable community-driven initiative, the villagers, School Management Committee (SMC), alumni, and village panchayat leaders have unanimously resolved to revive the Bui Upper Primary School in Gusar circle of Upper Subansiri district. The school had remained defunct for several years due to the absence of student enrolment. […]

item.title: Army subedar dies in road accident
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: [ Prem Chetry ] DIRANG, 26 Jun: In a tragic road accident, an Army subedar lost his life, while two others were injured near the PT ground of the 30 Bn SSB here in West Kameng at around 2 pm on Friday. Upon receiving information about the accident, the rapid rescue team of the 30 […]

item.title: International Day Against Drug Abuse observed
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: Mein urges citizens to join fight against drugs ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: The International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking was observed across the state on Friday to raise awareness on substance abuse and strengthen efforts for a drug-free society. Deputy Chief Minister Chowna Mein urged every citizen of the state to join the fight […]

item.title: Sanitary supervisor held for allegedly filming women in hospital
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: NAHARLAGUN, 26 Jun: A sanitary supervisor at the Tomo Riba Institute of Health and Medical Sciences (TRIHMS) here was arrested for allegedly filming women inside a hospital washroom using a mobile phone, police said on Friday. The accused, identified as Tamchi Nido (29), a resident of Kurung Kumey district, had been working as a multitasking […]

item.title: 14 stolen two-wheelers recovered, 4 minors taken into custody
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: The Arunachal Pradesh Police have recovered 14 stolen two-wheelers and taken four minors into custody in this connection. Capital Superintendent of Police Jummar Basar said a coordinated operation was launched after a theft case was registered at the Itanagar police station on Thursday. After a case was registered, police apprehended four children […]

item.title: KDS member Bomju Riram selected for diploma course at NSD
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: Bomju Riram, a member of the Kamsa Dramatics Society (KDS), has been selected for the prestigious three-year diploma in dramatic arts course at the National School of Drama (NSD), New Delhi, for the 2026-29academic session. A native of Basar in Leparada district, Bomju is the daughter of Karbom Riram and Gumbi Riram. […]

item.title: DCC, DLRC meeting reviews banking performance
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: DAPORIJO, 26 Jun: A District Consultative Committee (DCC) and District Level Review Committee (DLRC) meeting was held here on Friday under the chairmanship of Upper Subansiri Deputy Commissioner Tasso Gambo. The meeting brought together representatives from banks, the NABARD, line departments, and other stakeholders to review the district’s banking performance, credit flow, and implementation of […]

item.title: Aya, Rokpo and Dodo segments declared community land
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: BASAR, 26 Jun: Aya, Rokpo and Dodo segments in Leparada district were declared as community land of Disi village under customary laws and traditions of the Galo community during the Disi village general assembly (lunv banv) held on 19 June. The village’s general assembly, held under the chairmanship GB Sento Riba, has also demarcated boundaries […]

item.title: Planning authority reviews draft GIS-based master plan
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: KHONSA, 26 Jun: The Khonsa Planning Authority on Wednesday held a meeting of the Local Planning Authority (LPA) at the deputy commissioner’s conference hall here in Tirap district to review the draft GIS-based master plan-2051 for Khonsa under the AMRUT Scheme 2.0. The meeting, chaired by Deputy Commissioner Techu Aran, deliberated on the roadmap for […]

item.title: Prog under Khet Bachao Abhiyan held
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: TAROYAR, 26 Jun: Thirty-four farmers and farmwomen participated in a Khet Bachao Abhiyan programme organised here in Pakke-Kessang district by Pampoli-based East Kameng Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) on Friday. Organised in collaboration with the district agriculture office (DAO) team, an intensive awareness-cum-training programme on natural farming was conducted. The technical sessions were led by a […]

item.title: Miss Universe Arunachal 1st runner-up Peyang welcomed home
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: [ Mingkeng Osik ] PASIGHAT, 26 Jun: Well-wishers, friends and supporters accorded a warm reception to Miss Universe Arunachal Pradesh 2026 1st runner-up and Miss Multimedia title-holder Anchali Peyang upon her arrival here in East Siang district. Peyang, the reigning Miss Lune 2025, has emerged as one of the state’s most promising young beauty pageant […]

item.title: Forum places grievances before land mgmt commissioner
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: The Kamala Dam Affected People’s Forum (KDAPF-K3), representing the project-affected families (PAFs) of Kamle, Kra Daadi and Kurung Kumey districts, has sought the intervention of the land management commissioner in resolving the grievances of the PAFs regarding the proposed Kamala hydroelectric project (1,720 mw). In a representation to the land management commissioner, […]

item.title: Vocational trg prog on scientific poultry farming
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: ITANAGAR, 26 Jun: More than 30 trainees participated in a three-day vocational training programme on scientific poultry farming conducted by the Papum Pare KVK, which concluded on Friday. During the inaugural session, KVK Head Dr Bangkeng Perme advised the participants to venture into poultry farming industry and not depend on supply from other states. Dr […]

item.title: Miao power sub-station set for commissioning by July-end
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: [ Pisi Zauing ] MIAO, 26 Jun: The authorities of the Power Grid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL) have informed that the much-awaited 132/33 kv power sub-station here in Changlang district is scheduled to be commissioned by the end of July this year. The project, which has witnessed several delays over the years, is finally […]

item.title: Libang reviews NABARD-funded projects
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: YINGKIONG, 26 Jun: MLA Alo Libang reviewed the progress of all ongoing NABARD-funded projects in Upper Siang district during a meeting with Shivaji Munshi from the NABARD here on Friday. They also discussed potential new developmental activities that may be undertaken in the region. Special focus was given to exploring avenues for assistance under the […]

item.title: Lessons from the past
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) has responded to social media posts and news reports alleging inflation of land compensation estimates for the Kamle hydroelectric project. No stranger to investigating cases of inflated land compensation, the bureau has stressed that transparency, fairness, and due diligence must guide the assessment and disbursement of compensation. Videos and photographs circulating […]

item.title: Time for strategy not fallacy
item.dc:creator: author
item.description: Facing Economic Shock By Dhurjati Mukherjee The outlook for the Indian economy in the current fiscal sadly does not appear quite encouraging, despite growing 7.8% in January-March quarter of the last fiscal. The Reserve Bank of India left the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% but reduced its growth forecast for FY27 and raised its inflation […]
